title: socks5_helper: no NO_AUTH method validation in greeting
description:
In socks5_helper.rs lines 34-41, the greeting handler reads the NMETHODS count and the methods bytes, but never checks whether NO_AUTH (0x00) is present in the methods list. It unconditionally replies [0x05, 0x00] (selecting NO_AUTH) regardless of what the client actually offered.

Per RFC 1928 §3, if the server finds no acceptable method among those offered, it must reply [0x05, 0xFF] ('no acceptable methods') and close the connection.

A client that sends only username/password auth (0x02) and never offers NO_AUTH would get [0x05, 0x00] back, believe no-auth was negotiated, and proceed. This would mask a bug in a client implementation that sends wrong auth methods. The helper should assert that 0x00 is present in methods[], and ideally also test what happens when it is not (i.e., that transport::connect() fails).
